subject: Music
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 1.5 MELODY
topics: This topic is broken down into two subtopics which are: 1.5.1 Melodic motion and 1.5.2 Simple
Melody is a fundamental element of music often considered the most memorable part of a song
activities: Problem solving: Ability to use music to identify, analyse and address social-economical
Antecedent: The first part of a melodic phrase that sets up a musical idea, often ending
in uncertainty, to be resolved by the consequent.
Conjunct motion: Smooth, stepwise movement between, consecutive pitches in
assessment: z Provide learners with printed melodies and ask them to identify and label they valleys,
z Give learners a melody with missing notes and ask them to complete the melody by
adding notes that create a specific contour.
z Play a series of melodies with different contours and ask learners to identify the type
of contour used in each melody.
